source_jobs.title: Founding Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring a Founding Engineer to join an early-stage company building AI-powered software and data infrastructure for healthcare organizations operating in highly regulated, data-intensive environments.

This is a foundational engineering hire. You'll work directly with the founders to design and build core product experiences, develop AI-enabled workflows, implement customer deployments, and help establish the engineering systems and practices that will support the company's growth. The platform brings together large volumes of clinical, operational, and financial data to automate workflows, surface actionable insights, and improve decision-making for enterprise healthcare customers.

The role is well suited for an engineer who enjoys working across product, design, and engineering, is comfortable wearing multiple hats, and thrives owning projects from initial concept through production.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN


PRODUCT ENGINEERING

 - Design, build, and ship product features across the stack, from database design and APIs through frontend applications

 - Build and maintain integrations with external systems, structured datasets, and enterprise data sources

 - Develop infrastructure that powers AI-enabled product experiences, including LLM-driven workflows and intelligent automation

 - Improve application performance, reliability, and scalability as customer usage and data volumes grow

 - Work closely with founders and users to translate complex operational requirements into intuitive software


CUSTOMER DEPLOYMENTS

 - Partner with customers during implementation and onboarding

 - Configure integrations, data mappings, and deployment workflows

 - Participate in customer conversations and translate feedback into product improvements

 - Support and maintain the systems you build, including incident response, debugging, and operational troubleshooting


ENGINEERING FOUNDATION

 - Help establish engineering practices around testing, deployments, observability, security, and code review

 - Build systems that meet the reliability and compliance expectations of enterprise customers

 - Mentor future team members and help shape the company's engineering culture




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 3–5+ years of experience building and shipping customer-facing software

 - Familiarity with healthcare data standards (FHIR, HL7, X12, EHR, claims, or similar)

 - Strong experience with React, TypeScript, and modern web application development

 - Full-stack experience across frontend applications, APIs, databases, and background services, including work with complex enterprise or operational data systems

 - Experience building and deploying AI-enabled product features using modern LLMs, agent frameworks, or retrieval-based systems

 - Familiarity with prompt engineering, evaluation workflows, and AI application development best practices

 - Strong product judgment and the ability to work effectively with limited process or oversight

Bonus

 - Experience in customer-facing engineering roles such as implementation engineering, solutions engineering, forward deployed engineering, or technical consulting

 - Understanding of enterprise deployment requirements, security best practices, and compliance-focused environments (HIPAA, SOC 2, etc.)

 - Comfortable working in a fast-paced startup where engineering, product, and customer success are closely intertwined




BENEFITS

The starting salary for this role is $180,000, with a range up to $240,000 USD. The provided salary depends on many factors, such as work experience and transferable skills, business needs and impact, and market demands.




ABOUT OUR CLIENT

Our client is an early-stage, venture-backed software company building an AI-native platfor

source_jobs.title: Founding Marketer
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring the first dedicated Marketing hire at a Series A AI infrastructure company, someone who will work directly alongside the CEO to build the brand, the content engine, and the go-to-market narrative from the ground up. The CEO plans to do meaningful IC marketing work himself. This isn't a "hire someone and hand it off" situation — it's a genuine partnership.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Product Marketing — Develop positioning, messaging, and go-to-market strategies. Clearly communicate the technical value to the people who need to buy it.

 - Content Strategy — Build a content engine that educates developers and establishes the company as a credible voice in the market.

 - Thought Leadership — Elevate founder and team voices across social media, events, and industry channels.

 - Demand Generation — Design programs that drive awareness and generate pipeline.

 - Brand Development — Shape the company’s presence in the market as it scales.

 - Events & Community — Support CTO dinners, developer events, industry conferences, and buyer/vendor programs.

 - Sales Enablement — Create collateral, competitive talk tracks, and messaging frameworks that strengthen the go-to-market team.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 4+ years in product marketing, growth marketing, or content marketing in a B2B environment

 - Real experience marketing to technical buyers — engineers, VPs of Product, developer audiences

 - Strong product marketing fundamentals: positioning, messaging, competitive differentiation, launch

 - Exceptional writing — you can make a complex technical product feel clear and worth paying attention to

 - Experience building content that earns attention rather than just filling a calendar

 - Comfort being the first and only marketer — you build the system, not just run in it

 - Experience in developer tools, APIs, infrastructure software, or AI products is a strong plus




ABOUT OUR CLIENT

This venture backed AI infrastructure company is developing the connectivity layer that enables AI platforms and B2B software to take meaningful action within the systems businesses rely on. The founding team brings experience from Google, Meta, and AWS. The team is small, retention rates are exceptional, and the technical standards are high. This is an excellent opportunity for professionals who are excited about meaningful ownership and the chance to help shape the future of collaboration between businesses and AI.

source_jobs.title: Don't see a fit? Let us know more about you
source_jobs.description:
GENERAL APPLICATION

If you're excited about joining a venture-backed company but don't see a specific open role that matches your background, we encourage you to submit a general application. We work with a diverse portfolio of client companies with evolving hiring needs, and we're always looking to connect talented individuals with the right opportunities.

When you apply, please share:

 - Your ideal role: What type of position are you seeking? (e.g., engineering, product, operations, people/HR, recruiting, sales, marketing, etc.)

 - Your experience and strengths: What are your key skills and areas of expertise?

 - Company preferences: What stage, size, industry, or mission resonates most with you?

 - What excites you: What kind of team culture, values, or working environment helps you do your best work?

This information helps us understand which client opportunities might be the best match for your interests and abilities. As roles open up that align with your profile, we'll reach out to connect you with the right companies. Your application also helps us better understand the talent landscape and stay connected to exceptional candidates like you.




ABOUT PCT

People Culture Talent helps VCs and venture-backed startups build thriving teams through exceptional Human Capital Management. We create people-first talent strategies for high-growth companies, partnering with organizations from pre-seed to IPO to transform their growth into a masterclass in scaling with purpose.




ABOUT OUR CLIENT COMPANIES

PCT partners with exceptional venture-backed companies to build resilient, people-centric, diverse teams. Our clients span industries and stages, from pre-seed startups to companies preparing for IPO, including leading tech organizations like Notion, Lyft, Modern Health, Instacart, Uber, Google, Render, and GitHub.

These companies share a commitment to building strong culture, high-performing teams, and innovative people programs. They're growing fast, scaling thoughtfully, and seeking exceptional talent to join their journey.

source_jobs.title: Founding Talent Partner
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!



THE ROLE

We're hiring a Founding Talent Partner to build and scale recruiting at a fast growing stealth robotics company. This is the first dedicated recruiting hire and an opportunity to build the talent function from the ground up.

You'll partner directly with founders and senior leaders to define hiring strategy, design recruiting processes, and attract exceptional talent across technical, operational, and leadership functions. As the organization enters a period of accelerated growth, you'll play a critical role in shaping both the team and the candidate experience.

We're looking for someone who combines strategic recruiting instincts with operational excellence and genuine grit. You'll be equally comfortable building sourcing strategies, partnering with hiring managers on talent calibration, and personally driving searches for highly competitive candidates.

If you're energized by ambiguity and can craft fresh playbooks when old ones fall short, enjoy building new systems while offering strategic guidance, and are excited to evolve in a high growth environment, we’d love to connect.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Own Full Cycle Recruiting — Lead hiring across technical, GTM, operations, and leadership roles simultaneously.

 - Strategic Sourcing — Go beyond traditional, volume driven sourcing to identify overlooked talent, build lasting relationships, and create repeatable sourcing strategies.

 - Leadership Partnership — Collaborate with founders and hiring managers to clarify hiring priorities, align on ideal candidate profiles, and build a structured, effective interview process.

 - Employer Brand — Shape the employer brand and candidate experience, from initial outreach through offer.

 - ATS & System Optimization — Own and optimize our ATS infrastructure (Ashby), scorecards, and pipeline analytics to ensure a scalable hiring process.

 - Hiring Health & Insights — Monitor funnel health (pass-through rates, time-to-hire, rejection themes) and turn those insights into better, faster hiring decisions.

 - Team Leadership — Coach current stakeholders and, as the company scales, hire and develop a recruiting team.
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Bring 6+ years of full cycle recruiting, preferably in fast moving startups or technical environments.

 - Have built or significantly shaped a recruiting function and can define the bar while scaling it.

 - Excel at generating pipelines early: you uncover overlooked candidates and use creative approaches to keep funnels full.

 - Work comfortably with modern tools and AI enabled workflows, using them to increase leverage and speed without sacrificing judgment.

 - Show grit, humility, and steady maturity: you give candid feedback when needed, stay composed, and protect the candidate experience.

 - Learn quickly and create new playbooks when the old ones don’t apply.

 - Communicate crisply with a strong, client-facing style; fluent written and spoken English.

 - Able to collaborate on Pacific Time, with occasional evening or weekend conversations as needed.

 - Background in deep tech and/or hardware recruiting is a bonus, not a requirement.
   


WHY JOIN NOW

 - A Rare Stage of Growth — The company has achieved meaningful traction and recently secured a $100M Series A, creating a unique opportunity to join at a moment where your work will directly influence how the company operates and grows as it enters its next chapter.

 - Real Impact — You'll join a team focused on solving complex challenges with technology that is already delivering measurable value in live environments.

 - Work Alongside Exceptional People — The team is composed of experienced builders and technical leaders who care deeply about their craft and maintain a high bar for execution.

source_jobs.title: Founding Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring a Founding Product Manager to fill a gap that's been founder-owned until now at a Series A AI infrastructure company. You'll work alongside the CTO, a founding engineering team, and in close contact with customers to define what gets built and why. The role requires genuine technical fluency, strong customer instincts, and the ability to operate with minimal structure.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Product Strategy — Define and drive the roadmap. Make sure engineering investment is pointed at the right problems.

 - Customer Discovery — Talk to customers directly and often. Surface the patterns that should shape product direction.

 - Roadmap Prioritization — Balance customer feedback, technical constraints, and business goals. Make the call and document the reasoning.

 - Cross-Functional Leadership — Connect engineering, leadership, and go-to-market. Keep priorities aligned as the company scales.

 - Product Development — Guide initiatives from concept to launch. Stay close to the work without micromanaging it.

 - Market Insight — Track competitive dynamics and industry trends.

 - Operational Foundation — Stand up the product processes, planning rhythms, and communication structures that don't exist yet.
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 4+ years of product management experience in B2B SaaS, developer tools, infrastructure, or enterprise software

 - Experience working directly with engineering teams to ship technical products

 - Strong customer discovery instincts — you find signal in conversations, not just data

 - Technical fluency — you can engage credibly with engineers on architecture and implementation tradeoffs

 - Excellent written communication; decisions travel in writing on an async-first team

 - A data-informed approach to prioritization — you can defend your calls

 - Experience with APIs, integrations, or infrastructure products is a strong plus
   


ABOUT OUR CLIENT

This venture backed AI infrastructure company is developing the connectivity layer that enables AI platforms and B2B software to take meaningful action within the systems businesses rely on. The founding team brings experience from Google, Meta, and AWS. The team is small, retention rates are exceptional, and the technical standards are high. This is an excellent opportunity for professionals who are excited about meaningful ownership and the chance to help shape the future of collaboration between businesses and AI.

source_jobs.title: Founding Business Development Lead
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring a Founding Business Development Lead to join an early stage healthcare technology company building software and data infrastructure for health plans and other risk-bearing healthcare organizations.

This is a foundational go-to-market hire. You'll work directly with the founders to build the company's outbound motion, develop relationships with senior healthcare leaders, and generate qualified pipeline within a defined set of target accounts. The role is well suited for someone who enjoys building processes from the ground up, thrives in a founder-led sales environment, and has experience selling into complex healthcare organizations.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Account Strategy – Build and manage target accounts across health plans, managed care organizations, and risk bearing provider groups. Develop account intelligence, identify key stakeholders, and prioritize opportunities based on fit and timing.

 - Pipeline Generation – Generate qualified executive meetings through outbound outreach, referrals, industry networks, and events. Build relationships across buying committees, lead initial discovery conversations, and maintain a healthy pipeline.

 - Industry Engagement – Represent the company at healthcare conferences and industry events. Coordinate pre-event outreach, support customer meetings, and drive follow-up activities that convert conversations into opportunities.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 3–5+ years of experience in business development, sales development, or pipeline generation within healthcare technology, preferably as an early go-to-market hire at a healthcare technology company.

 - Direct experience engaging with leaders in analytics, finance, operations, quality, population health, or strategy within payer organizations.

 - Experience working closely with founders, executives, or senior sales leadership to develop and refine repeatable commercial processes.

 - Proven ability to generate qualified opportunities for enterprise software, analytics, value-based care, population health, revenue cycle, clinical technology, or related solutions.

 - Demonstrated success in sourcing executive-level meetings and building a pipeline through outbound initiatives.

 - Strong understanding of healthcare payer organizations and their operating environment.

 - Familiarity with concepts related to healthcare performance, quality, risk, and financial management.

 - Proficiency with modern sales engagement, prospecting, research, and CRM platforms.

 - Excellent written communication skills and the ability to tailor messaging to senior executives.

 - Comfort operating in a founder-led sales environment where responsibilities change rapidly and ownership is expected.
   


COMPENSATION BAND

The starting salary for this role is $140,000, with a range up to $180,000 USD. The provided salary depends on many factors, such as work experience and transferable skills, business needs and impact, and market demands.




ABOUT OUR CLIENT

Our client is an early-stage, venture-backed software company building an AI-native platform for organizations managing complex operational and healthcare data. Their technology helps customers unify fragmented information, automate manual workflows, and generate actionable insights that improve decision-making across clinical and business operations.

The founding team brings deep expertise across software engineering, machine learning, and enterprise systems, and is building a category-defining platform for one of the largest and most data-intensive industries. This is an opportunity to join at the earliest stages, work directly alongside the founders, and help shape both the product and engineering organization from the ground up.

source_jobs.title: Founding Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're seeking one of our first Account Executives to manage the full sales cycle across enterprise and growth accounts. You'll play a key role in securing new customers and collaborating to build our sales playbook from scratch. This position is ideal for someone who brings a founder's mindset—taking initiative and influencing everything from sales strategy to product feedback. In our small, agile team, everyone takes on multiple roles, so we're looking for someone ready to make a substantial impact on the company's success and future direction.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Drive revenue growth by managing the entire sales cycle, from initial outreach to contract execution, across both enterprise and growth stage customers.

 - Create and optimize sales processes by developing scalable frameworks for discovery, qualification, technical validation, and procurement.

 - Lead technical conversations by engaging confidently with engineering leaders and technical stakeholders.

 - Cultivate internal champions by navigating complex buying committees and guiding opportunities through security, procurement, and approval processes.

 - Collaborate with leadership by partnering closely with founders and GTM leaders on account planning and strategic deal execution.

 - Shape product direction by serving as a critical voice of the customer, translating market feedback into actionable product insights.
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of full-cycle B2B SaaS sales experience, including at least 2 years selling technical products to technical buyers

 - A proven track record of consistently meeting or exceeding quota

 - AI fluency—you use AI tools regularly and can speak credibly with buyers building AI-powered products

 - Technically confident with integrations, APIs, OAuth, webhooks, and understanding architectural tradeoffs

 - Excellent communication skills and strong writing ability

 - Possess a builder's mentality—thriving in ambiguity and driven to develop the sales playbook from the ground up.

 - Vertical Fit:
   
   - GTM stack — HubSpot, CRM platforms, revenue tooling buyers
   
   - Fintech stack — payments infrastructure, Square ecosystem, ERP/fintech buyers
   
   Even Better If You:
   
   - Served as an early AE (one of the first 1–3) at a Seed or Series A company that scaled
   
   - Have a technical background, such as a CS degree, experience as a former SE, or have launched your own software project
   
   - Have built or shipped an AI workflow or agent




ABOUT OUR CLIENT

This venture backed AI infrastructure company is developing the connectivity layer that enables AI platforms and B2B software to take meaningful action within the systems businesses rely on. The founding team brings experience from Google, Meta, and AWS. The team is small, retention rates are exceptional, and the technical standards are high. source_jobs.title: Founding Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're looking for a Founding Engineering Manager to join a Series A AI infrastructure company at a pivotal moment. You'll work directly with the founders and a founding engineering team that came up through Google, Meta, Cruise, and AWS. This team writes their own feature docs, owns customer support rotations, and holds craft as a non-negotiable value. The people here haven't left. There is high retention, high trust, and this person's job will be to protect that culture while scaling it.

This is a hands-on role. You'll be close to the technical work, close to the people doing it, and close to the decisions that shape how the team grows.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Engineering Leadership — Mentor and develop a high retention team of engineers. Build the conditions where strong people do their best work and stay.

 - Team Scaling — Partner with founders on hiring, onboarding, and career development as the engineering org grows beyond its founding core.

 - Technical Execution — Own planning and delivery across multiple initiatives. Hold the bar on quality and system design without slowing the team down.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration — Connect engineering to product and customer needs. Make sure the team is building the right things, not just building things well.

 - Process & Ops — Build operating rhythms that actually help — lightweight and practical for a team that doesn't need heavy process to produce.

 - Technical Strategy — Contribute to architectural decisions and long-term platform investments that support scale without accumulating debt.

 - Customer Impact — Stay close to customer facing engineers. Understand the real world problems the platform is solving and make sure solutions reflect that.
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 5+ years of software engineering experience, including 2+ years managing engineering teams in a startup or high-growth environment

 - A track record of shipping complex technical work and developing the engineers around you

 - Strong technical judgment — you can go deep on architecture and system design when it matters

 - Experience building engineering culture, not just org charts — ownership, accountability, and craft over process

 - Excellent written communication; comfortable in an async-first environment where decisions are documented and context travels in writing

 - A customer-centric instinct and genuine interest in how the platform performs in production

 - Experience with AI-native products, developer tools, infrastructure platforms, or agent-based systems is a strong plus

 - Nice to Have:
   
   - Background in developer tooling, APIs, or cloud infrastructure
   
   - Experience in customer-facing engineering organizations
   
   - Early engineering leadership at a startup (first or second management hire
     


ABOUT OUR CLIENT

This venture backed AI infrastructure company is developing the connectivity layer that enables AI platforms and B2B software to take meaningful action within the systems businesses rely on. The founding team brings experience from Google, Meta, and AWS. The team is small, retention rates are exceptional, and the technical standards are high. This is an excellent opportunity for professionals who are excited about meaningful ownership and the chance to help shape the future of collaboration between businesses and AI.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

As one of our first Sales Development Representatives, you'll take ownership of building and scaling the top of our sales funnel across both enterprise and growth markets. Your primary focus will be creating new business opportunities through strategic prospecting and innovative outreach across a variety of channels. This role is ideal for someone who thrives on experimentation, embraces emerging AI technologies, and views an empty pipeline as an opportunity rather than a challenge.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Drive pipeline growth through outbound prospecting across both enterprise and growth accounts.

 - Secure meetings by utilizing a mix of channels—including LinkedIn, cold calling, email, events, and creative outreach strategies.

 - Run experiments by treating sales as an iterative process: test ideas, analyze results, and double down on what proves effective.

 - Conduct account research by identifying and building highly targeted prospect lists, leveraging tools like Claude, Clay, and AI-powered workflows.

 - Execute multi-channel campaigns by combining traditional outbound methods with innovative approaches.

 - Partner closely with AEs and founders to support account planning, discovery preparation, and expansion opportunities.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 1–2 years of full-time sales experience in a quota-carrying or customer-facing role, ideally within a SaaS environment

 - Strong AI proficiency — you regularly use AI tools in your daily workflow and are enthusiastic about enhancing productivity through AI

 - Excellent communication skills — you are a strong writer and confident engaging with senior stakeholders

 - A curious mindset — you enjoy learning about new technologies and understanding customer challenges

 - A competitive, self-driven attitude — you are motivated by ambitious goals and eager to increase your impact

 - Technical adaptability — you quickly become comfortable learning new software and systems

This venture backed AI infrastructure company is developing the connectivity layer that enables AI platforms and B2B software to take meaningful action within the systems businesses rely on. source_jobs.title: Founding Member of Technical Staff
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

Healthcare is at its greatest inflection point. Systems are adopting technology faster than ever before, and Dart Health is leading that charge on the clinical side.

We're hiring our first Member of Technical Staff in NYC — a founding engineer who will work shoulder-to-shoulder with our founders to scale the platform, shape product direction, and help build the NYC engineering team from the ground up. This is a role with significant leadership scope from day one and a clear path into a Head of Engineering seat as the company grows.

You'll be ~50% hands-on across architecture and code, ~50% partnering with the founders on product, R&D, and team building. If you're early in your career but ready to operate at the level of a founding engineer — or further along and ready to step into more leadership — we want to talk.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Build and ship — Spend ~50% of your time as a hands-on contributor: designing architectures, writing code, reviewing PRs, and solving hard technical problems across our stack

 - Shape the platform — Lead architecture decisions for enterprise-grade healthcare deployments, with meaningful ownership

 - Partner with the founders — Drive product direction and R&D alongside Veeraj and Aakruti, including our virtual hospital simulation lab, a synthetic patient R&D environment that will change how we test and validate clinical AI

 - Help build the team — Collaborate with the existing engineering team in São Paulo (6 engineers) and play a key role in recruiting and growing the NYC team as we scale from ~11 to 15–20 this year

 - Set the bar — Own quality, reliability, and speed in a trust-critical environment where technology reaches clinicians in weeks, not quarters

 - Grow into leadership — There's a clear path into a Head of Engineering role for the right person as the team scales




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 4–10 years of engineering experience with deep, hands-on ownership of complex technical systems

 - Currently spending ~50%+ of your time as a hands-on contributor — you're a builder first

 - Founding engineer or former founder energy — you've operated with high autonomy, owned ambiguous problems end-to-end, and don't need process to get things done

 - First-principles thinking and the ability to operate in high-variability, trust-critical environments

 - Comfortable being in-person 5 days a week in our Chelsea office, with the energy to move fast

 - Forward Deployed Engineer (FDE) experience is a strong plus — the ability to sit at the intersection of customer problems and technical solutions is exactly what this role calls for

 - No healthcare background required; curiosity about clinical AI and patient impact is what matters







WHY JOIN NOW

 - Direct patient impact — the work you do will affect how care is delivered to hundreds of thousands of people

 - Founding-team scope — real agency to propose and build new products using customer data, clinicians, and internal R&D infrastructure

 - The hardest, most interesting problems in applied clinical AI, including building a virtual simulation lab for synthetic patient testing the industry has been working toward for decades

 - A clear leadership path — this role is designed to grow into a Head of Engineering seat for the right person

 - A small, high-trust team that moves with urgency and celebrates wins together

 - Backed by market leaders, including Mayo Clinic, 43.io http://43.io, and Virtue VC, who trust Dart to build and deploy safe AI across a multitude of use cases




COMPENSATION BAND

The starting salary for this role is $250,000 USD, with a range up to $350,000 USD, plus strong pre-seed equity. The provided salary depends on many factors, such as work experience and transferabl

source_jobs.title: Infrastructure Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!




The Role

Arena Intelligence is the open platform for evaluating how AI models perform in the real world. Born out of UC Berkeley’s SkyLab and the team behind Arena.ai http://Arena.ai, our leaderboards are the industry’s gold standard for AI model evaluation — trusted by researchers, developers, and enterprises shaping the future of AI.

We’re building the API-based services for enterprise customers— We want to provide enterprises with the best of the ML products (like Arena Max) but customized to their business.

You’ll own core infrastructure that turns our research advantage into an enterprise products. This is a founding role on the developer/enterprise team. You’ll work directly with the founders and early customers to define what we build and how we build it.



What You’ll Do

• Build API based products from the ground up. Design and implement a low-latency, high-reliability APIs for leaderboards, models, and arenas.

• Solve hard streaming problems. Handle SSE/streaming responses across heterogeneous providers, including partial failure recovery, mid-stream fallback, and consistent response normalization.

• Ship enterprise-grade infrastructure. Build the systems enterprise customers expect: rate limiting, authentication, usage metering, cost attribution, audit logging, and SOC2 compliance

• Build deep observability. Instrument infrastructure with distributed tracing, latency breakdowns, token-level usage tracking, and real-time dashboards so customers (and we) can see exactly what’s happening.

• Build AI centered products. Integrate with our core evaluation platform, Arena data, and customer-specific benchmarks. Collaborate with the research team to turn novel ideas into full-featured products

• Flex across the stack. Contribute to the backend of our Leaderboards and Evals platforms when needed, helping unify our public and private data architectures.



What We’re Looking For

• 4+ years of backend engineering experience, with meaningful time spent on distributed systems, infrastructure, or developer-facing platforms.

• Strong proficiency in Go and/or Rust, with hands-on experience building high-throughput APIs or proxy/gateway systems.

• Experience with LLM provider APIs (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, etc.) and a working understanding of the challenges: streaming, token management, rate limits, model-specific quirks.

• Solid cloud infrastructure skills — you’re comfortable with AWS or GCP, Kubernetes, Terraform, and database systems like Postgres and Redis.

• A product-oriented mindset. You think about the developer experience of your APIs, not just the implementation. You ask “why” before “how.”

• Comfort with ambiguity. We’re a startup. Scope is fluid, context shifts, and you’ll wear many hats. That should sound exciting, not stressful.



Nice to Have

• Experience building API gateways, proxies, or developer tools (Bifrost, Kong, Envoy, Tyk, or custom).

• Background in ML infrastructure, model serving, or evaluation frameworks.

• Experience building enterprise-ready features: SSO, RBAC, audit logs, multi-tenancy.

• Familiarity with the modern AI infra stack (vLLM, LiteLLM, LangChain, etc.).



COMPENSATION BAND

Their openings span more than one career level. The starting salary for this role is $200k and could range up to $350k USD, plus equity. The provided salary depends on many factors, such as work experience and transferable skills, business needs and impact, and market demands.




BENEFITS

 - Comprehensive health, dental, vision, and additional support programs.

 - The opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI with a small, mission-driven team.

 - A culture that values transparency, trust, and community impact.

 - Visa sponsorship available.




ABOUT OUR CLIENT:

Ar

source_jobs.title: Deployment Strategist
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring a Deployment Strategist to join a fast growing healthcare AI company that is transforming how clinical teams monitor and care for patients at scale. This role sits at the intersection of technical implementation and product development. You'll own the deployment lifecycle from post-sale onboarding through long term adoption, ensuring customers realize measurable value while helping shape the future of the platform.

You'll work directly with clinical leaders and internal engineering teams to implement solutions and drive adoption, while uncovering opportunities for product improvement. Every deployment presents unique challenges, making this an ideal opportunity for someone who thrives in a dynamic environment and is energized about partnering with diverse stakeholders to solve high impact clinical and operational problems. As the company enters its next phase of growth, you'll play a key role in helping scale implementations across some of the most innovative healthcare organizations in the country.

If you enjoy taking ownership of complex customer challenges and want a seat at the table as a high growth company scales, we'd love to connect.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Client Deployment & Onboarding — Lead full cycle implementations, ensuring successful onboarding and adoption across customer organizations.

 - Implementation Strategy — Partner with customer leadership teams to define deployment plans and rollout strategies.

 - Experience managing client relationships and driving projects from kickoff through successful execution.

 - Comfort working with data and analytical tools; familiarity with SQL is a plus.

 - Strong problem solving skills and the ability to navigate ambiguity in fast changing environments.

 - A high ownership mindset with a willingness to roll up your sleeves and solve problems wherever needed.

 - Experience in startups or high growth environments is strongly preferred.

 - Healthcare experience is helpful but not required.
   


WHY JOIN NOW

 - Product Market Fit Already Established — The company has built a proven solution that is already delivering measurable value for customers in live environments.

 - Meaningful Growth Trajectory — With more than $1M in annual recurring revenue achieved within its first year and strong market momentum, the company is entering an exciting phase of expansion.

 - Direct Impact on Healthcare — Your work will help healthcare organizations improve care delivery and operational efficiency at scale.

 - High Ownership Environment — You'll have significant autonomy and visibility, with opportunities to influence pro

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer Lead
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

This is a Senior Security Lead role designed to lead the strategy and hands-on implementation of systems that protect the integrity of Arena Intelligence, a multi-million user AI platform, shaping the future of how the world measures artificial intelligence. You’ll work across product, infrastructure, and data pipelines to proactively identify risks, embed security into core features, and build scalable defenses against abuse, fraud, and adversarial behavior.

This is a builder role - you’ll not only set technical direction but also write the code, build the tools, and design the frameworks that make security and trust part of this product’s DNA.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the product security vision, ensuring security and trust are core to every stage of the product lifecycle.

 - Design and implement platform-wide security features, including Sybil resistance, bot detection, reputation systems, and anti-abuse primitives.

 - Lead threat modeling and security architecture reviews for new and existing product features.

 - Collaborate with infrastructure and product engineering to design secure APIs, data flows, and identity systems that scale.

 - Improve developer velocity by creating secure-by-default frameworks and tooling for internal teams.

 - Partner with incident response to quickly assess, contain, and remediate security events, and lead deep postmortems to improve defenses.

 - Stay ahead of the curve by monitoring emerging attack techniques and applying cutting-edge security research to our platform.

 - Mentor engineers across the company on secure coding practices, architecture trade-offs, and operational security.




QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of experience in software engineering or security engineering, including staff-level scope in securing large-scale, user-facing platforms.

 - Proven track record designing and implementing systems to detect, mitigate, and prevent adversarial behavior (bots, Sybil attacks, automated abuse).

 - Strong experience with threat modeling, secure architecture design, and risk assessment.

 - Hands-on experience building security features into production systems at scale (millions of DAU / billions of requests).

 - Proficiency in backend development (Node.js, TypeScript, Python, or Go) and willingness to work across the stack when needed.

 - Strong knowledge of distributed systems security, identity, and authentication mechanisms.

 - Excellent communication skills, able to build alignment across engineering, product, and leadership teams.


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience in adversarial ML, trust & safety systems, or securing voting/reputation platforms.

 - Familiarity with advanced detection methods, user fingerprinting, or behavioral analytics.

 - Contributions to open-source security tools or research.

 - Background in securing real-time, interactive platforms at scale.

 - Built real-time detection systems and post-processing pipelines to identify attacks on large scale systems



COMPENSATION BAND

Their openings span more than one career level. The starting salary for this role is $200k and could range up to $400k USD, plus equity. The provided salary depends on many factors, such as work experience and transferable skills, business needs and impact, and market demands.




BENEFITS

 - Comprehensive health, dental, vision, and additional support programs.

 - The opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI with a small, mission-driven team.

 - A culture that values transparency, trust, and community impact.

 - Visa sponsorship available.




ABOUT OUR CLIENT:

Arena Intelligence is redefining what "better" means in AI. Built by researchers from UC Berkeley's SkyLab and backed by Felicis, Andreessen Horowitz, Kleiner Perkins, Light

source_jobs.title: People Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring a People Operations Lead to help build the foundation for how a fast growing technology company attracts and develops its team.

This is a rare opportunity to join at a stage where many of the most important people programs are still being defined. You'll work closely with leadership to create systems that scale, guide managers through critical people decisions, and shape the employee experience from the ground up. Reporting to the Head of People, you'll be the second dedicated People hire and a key partner in building a thoughtful, high performing organization. Success in this role requires someone who can move comfortably between strategic initiatives and hands on execution, bringing structure where it's needed without creating unnecessary process.

If you're energized by building, enjoy solving people challenges in real time, and want meaningful ownership over how a company grows, we'd love to connect.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - People Program Design — Build and evolve foundational programs including onboarding, performance management, compensation, career development, and other initiatives that support company growth.

 - Manager & Leadership Partnership — Serve as a trusted advisor to leaders, providing guidance on team effectiveness, organizational design, employee relations, and people decisions.

 - Organizational Scaling — Create lightweight systems and processes that improve consistency while preserving speed and flexibility.

 - Culture & Employee Experience — Help shape an environment where employees can do their best work, driving initiatives that strengthen engagement, communication, and team health.

 - HR Operations & Compliance — Ensure the fundamentals are operating smoothly, from policies and documentation to compliance and employee lifecycle management.

 - Workforce Planning — Partner with leadership on headcount planning, organizational structure, and evolving team needs.

 - People Infrastructure — Identify gaps before they become bottlenecks, building the frameworks and tools needed to support a growing organization.
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 6+ years of experience building People programs in an early stage or high growth environment where adaptability and execution mattered as much as expertise.

 - Strong HR business partnering skills and the ability to translate business goals into practical people strategies.

 - Sound judgment when navigating sensitive employee situations, balancing empathy with accountability.

 - A builder's mindset. You enjoy creating systems from scratch and improving them as the organization evolves.

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity and making progress without waiting for perfect information.

 - A practical approach to People Operations that focuses on outcomes rather than process for process's sake.

 - Familiarity with modern AI tools and a curiosity about how technology can improve internal workflows is a plus.
   


WHY JOIN NOW

 - Build, Don't Inherit — You'll have a direct hand in creating the programs, systems, and practices that will shape the company's future.

 - Meaningful Influence — This role sits close to leadership, giving you visibility into key decisions and the opportunity to influence how the organization grows.

 - Broad Scope — You'll work across strategy, operations, employee experience, and organizational effectiveness rather than being confined to a single area of HR.

 - A Defining Growth Stage — The company has established a strong foundation and is investing in the people infrastructure needed to support its next chapter.
   


COMPENSATION BAND

The starting salary for this role is $170,000, with a range up to $275,000 USD. The provided salary depends on many factors, such as work experience and

source_jobs.title: Product Engineer, Platform
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring a Product Engineer to shape the user experience of a platform used by some of the most technically sophisticated teams in software and AI.

This is a foundational role. You'll work closely with the founders, engineers, and customers to design experiences for complex, developer-facing workflows — and help establish the design standards the product will be built on from here. The team is small, the ownership is real, and the people you'll work with care deeply about craft. If you do your best work when the problems are hard and what you ship actually matters, then this is the environment for it.



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Full Stack Feature Development — Own the full loop: technical architecture, implementation, documentation, and customer communication. No throwing work over the fence. You will primarily work in the backend (Golang), but you are also comfortable implementing frontend features (React).

 - Customer Engagement — Talk directly to customers — support questions, feedback gathering. Let what you hear shape what you build.

 - API Design and Architecture — Every engineer proposes API designs and technical architecture for the features they work on. We write succinct design docs, generously give each other feedback, and push towards the best solution.

 - Scale and Reliability — With a rapidly scaling customer base, it is essential to build with scale and edge cases in mind. Every engineer contributes to building alerting, telemetry, internal tooling, and automation to help continue to ship fast while maintaining reliability.

 - AI Agents — You will work on planning, tool use, evals, observability, guardrails, and the infrastructure needed to make agentic workflows reliable for customers.
   


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 4+ years engineering and designing digital products, ideally in B2B SaaS, developer tools, enterprise software, or technical products

 - Golang and React experience is a strong plus

 - You are AI-forward and love trying new AI tools. You think of yourself not as a coder, but as a builder who harnesses AI to multiply their productivity.

 - Strong communication skills; you can articulate design decisions and push back when something isn't right. Engineers here write their own feature docs and own their decisions

 - Great taste in what makes developer tools intuitive; ideally you've shipped customer-facing developer products before

 - Strong product intuition — you can work directly with customers to design features, not just implement specs
   


ABOUT OUR CLIENT

This venture backed AI infrastructure company is developing the connectivity layer that enables AI platforms and B2B software to take meaningful action within the systems businesses rely on. The founding team brings experience from Google, Meta, and AWS. The team is small, retention rates are exceptional, and the technical standards are high. This is an excellent opportunity for professionals who are excited about meaningful ownership and the chance to help shape the future of collaboration between businesses and AI.

source_jobs.title: Frontend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring a Frontend Engineer to shape the user experience of a platform used by some of the most technically sophisticated teams in software and AI.

This is a foundational role. You'll work closely with the founders, engineers, and customers to design experiences for complex, developer-facing workflows — and help establish the design standards the product will be built on from here. The team is small, the ownership is real, and the people you'll work with care deeply about craft. If you do your best work when the problems are hard and what you ship actually matters, then this is the environment for it.

 


WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Dashboard — The dashboard is the command center that customers go to to manage and troubleshoot their integrations. It serves engineers, product managers, and customer enablement teams. You will work on the dashboard experience, including how it looks, feels, and functions.

 - UI library — The UI library is a set of embeddable React components and hooks that customers put into their application for their customers to set up their integrations. Because it is a part of our customer’s product experiences and frontend stack, there are unique technical and UX challenges to ensure that it is composable, flexible and seamlessly fit into our user’s UI.

 - UI Prototyping — You have the freedom to come up with idea and rapidly prototype them using AI tools. You don’t need to wait for mocks or get buy in from a designer. You need to have great taste and intuition.

 - Customer Engagement — Talk directly to customers — user testing sessions, support questions, feedback gathering. Let what you hear shape what you build.

 - Full Stack Feature Development — Own the full loop: design, implementation, documentation, and customer communication. No throwing work over the fence. You are not afraid to dive into the backend and make PRs to unblock yourself.

 - Frontend Performance — Our dashboard is data-intensive, and you will work to ensure that the experience feels snappy and delightful.


WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 4+ years engineering and designing digital products, ideally in B2B SaaS, developer tools, enterprise software, or technical products

 - Strong React skills with full-stack range (bonus if you have a genuine willingness to go deep in Go on the backend)

 - You are AI-forward and love trying new AI tools. You think of yourself not as a coder, but as a builder who harnesses AI to multiply their productivity.

 - Strong communication skills; you can articulate design decisions and push back when something isn't right. Engineers here write their own feature docs and own their decisions

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
Note: We are recruiting on behalf of our valued client. This opportunity is for a position with their organization, not with People Culture Talent. We're excited to help connect talented professionals with this exceptional team!





THE ROLE

We're hiring an Executive Assistant to support the founders and leadership team of a fast growing stealth robotics company entering an exciting new phase of growth.

As the company's first Executive Assistant, you'll establish the systems and operational rhythm that enable leadership to move at a fast pace without sacrificing organization or focus. The role requires strong judgment and high attention to detail. You’ll need to anticipate needs, prevent problems, and remove barriers before they impact the team. By building organized systems, you'll help ensure leaders remain aligned on the company's highest priorities without letting anything fall through the cracks.

This role is based in San Francisco and requires working onsite with the team five days per week, given how closely integrated it is with day-to-day operations. If you are highly organized, instinctively proactive, and excited to streamline how a high performing team operates, we’d love to connect.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Executive Support — Manage complex calendars for founding team, coordinating meetings with internal stakeholders, external partners, and cross functional teams through multiple timezones.

 - Stay Ahead of Priorities — Anticipate friction points and put simple processes in place to ensure priorities and details don’t slip

 - Communication Management — Manage the cofounders inbox and communications, triaging messages and drafting/responding as needed

 - Coordinate Travel & Logistics — Coordinate travel logistics, including flights, lodging, ground transportation, and detailed itineraries

 - Culture & Events — Organize and support events such as team dinners, offsites, and special occasions

 - Workplace Operations — Support office operations by collaborating with the Operations Manager on facilities logistics, vendor management, and inventory tracking.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - Experience supporting senior executives, founders, or leadership teams in a fast paced, high growth environment

 - A proactive mindset with the ability to anticipate needs, solve problems independently, and create structure in evolving situations

 - Exceptional organizational skills with a track record of managing competing priorities, coordinating complex schedules, and keeping projects on track

 - Strong judgment and discretion, with the ability to manage sensitive information and confidential matters with professionalism and care.

 - A high level of ownership and accountability, with a willingness to step in wherever needed to help the team succeed

THE ROLE

We are seeking a Developer Relations professional to strengthen our presence in the developer community by creating content, providing education, and authentically engaging with individuals building on our platform. This position is both technical and community focused. You will develop tutorials, speak at events, actively participate in developer communities, and act as a direct feedback channel between the developer ecosystem and our product team.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Technical Content — Produce tutorials, documentation, blog posts, videos, and demos that provide real value to developers.

 - Community Building — Engage in the spaces where developers are active. Build relationships, answer questions, and earn trust.

 - Product Feedback — Gather insights from developers and communicate them to the product team.

 - Developer Education — Share best practices, implementation strategies, and platform capabilities.

 - Event Participation — Represent the company at conferences, workshops, webinars, and community events.

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration — Collaborate closely with engineering, product, and marketing teams.




WHAT YOU’LL BRING

 - 3+ years in Developer Relations, developer advocacy, solutions engineering, or software engineering

 - Technical credibility — you can read and write code, and developer audiences will know it

 - Strong written and verbal communication; your content earns attention because it's genuinely good

 - Experience creating technical content that developers actually use
